steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence begins "Focusing on Mirounga angustirostris—or ______". An opening em-dash introduces a parenthetical; that parenthetical must be closed with a matching em-dash before the main clause can continue.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘northern elephant seals, as they're more commonly known, a’: Uses a comma to close, but the parenthetical was opened with an em-dash. Mixed punctuation breaks the pair. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘northern elephant seals, as they're more commonly known—a’: Closes the em-dash parenthetical with a second em-dash, then lets the main clause resume. Correctly matched pair. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘northern elephant seals, as they're more commonly known. A’: A period ends the sentence, leaving "Focusing on Mirounga angustirostris—" as a fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘northern elephant seals—as they're more commonly known, a’: Opens a second em-dash mid-parenthetical, creating two unmatched em-dashes and a comma that cannot close the first one. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
